hierarchy.h1: text-5xl sm:text-6xl tracking-tight leading-none font-normal uppercase
hierarchy.h2: text-3xl sm:text-4xl tracking-tight leading-none font-normal uppercase
hierarchy.label: text-xs font-bold uppercase tracking-[0.2em] text-accent

typography.guidelines: Use Bebas Neue exclusively for giant, scoreboard-style numbers, massive headers, and high-energy focal points. Use Manrope for everything else (UI components, lists, instructions). Never use generic sans like Inter for headings.

colors.guidelines: Cinematic 'stadium at night' palette. Avoid pure black; ground the UI in a deeply recessional near-black green. The volt lime green (#CCFF00) must be used sparingly, like stadium floodlights cutting through the dark—only for primary CTAs, active states, and live scores.

visual_enhancers.textures: Apply subtle film grain overlay on hero section backgrounds to simulate night photography and reduce digital flatness.
visual_enhancers.glassmorphism: Use backdrop-blur-xl paired with the surface_glass color and a 1px border_pitch stroke. This is mandatory for floating navigation and modal overlays.
visual_enhancers.pitch_motifs: Replace generic SaaS icons with geometric football motifs. A thin 1px white/10 vertical line for dividers. A hollow circle (border-white/10 rounded-full) placed in the absolute background of sections to mimic the center circle of a pitch.
visual_enhancers.shadows: Omit soft, diffuse shadows. Use sharp, glowing shadows for the accent color only, e.g., drop-shadow-[0_0_12px_rgba(204,255,0,0.4)] on the primary button.

buttons.primary: bg-[#CCFF00] text-black font-bold uppercase tracking-wider rounded-full px-8 py-4 hover:scale-105 transition-transform duration-200 shadow-[0_0_15px_rgba(204,255,0,0.3)]
buttons.secondary: border border-white/20 text-white bg-transparent rounded-full px-8 py-4 hover:border-[#CCFF00] hover:text-[#CCFF00] transition-colors duration-200 uppercase font-bold tracking-wider

cards.style: bg-[#0C1812] border border-white/10 rounded-none md:rounded-lg p-6 lg:p-8

inputs.style: bg-[#050A07] border-b border-white/20 text-white rounded-none px-4 py-3 focus:outline-none focus:border-[#CCFF00] transition-colors placeholder:text-white/30 font-body text-lg

icons.guideline: Strict minimal use of external icons. Favor custom geometric SVGs (lines, circles, dots). If a library must be used, use lucide-react but keep it abstract (e.g., Circle, Crosshair, ArrowRight). No smiley faces, people icons, or literal 'football' icons.

grids_and_layouts.mobile_first: Every screen must be functional on a small mobile device viewport first. Forms (Create Match, MVP voting) must be full-width on mobile with sticky bottom CTAs.
grids_and_layouts.bento_admin: Admin dashboard should use a dense, cardless grid layout where panels (players, payments, generate teams) sit tightly adjacent, separated by 1px white/10 borders (like pitch lines).
grids_and_layouts.spacing: Generous spacing logic: gap-8 to gap-12 between major vertical sections. Padding within match cards should be p-6.

motion.micro_interactions: Hover states on all clickable geometries. Slider for MVP voting should have smooth spring-physics tracking.
motion.page_transitions: Fade-in entrance for the hero section.
motion.team_generation: When teams are generated via snake draft, stagger the appearance of player names (stagger delay 0.1s) to build anticipation.

accessibility.contrast: Lime green text on black background achieves extreme contrast (WCAG AAA).
accessibility.focus: All interactive elements must display a 2px solid lime green ring (focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#CCFF00]) upon keyboard focus.
accessibility.testing: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute in kebab-case format (e.g., data-testid='create-match-button').

hero_background.description: Football stadium under lights at night. Use as a heavily darkened hero background with a radial gradient overlay fading to black at the edges.

player_action.description: A dramatic silhouette of a soccer player standing in rain with backlit lighting. Good for MVP or Player History sections.

instructions_to_main_agent: You are building FairXI: a mobile-first web app for casual football groups.
Implement the stadium at night theme: #050A07 background, #0C1812 surface cards, and #CCFF00 (Volt Lime) accents.
Install and use Bebas Neue for headings and Manrope for body text via Google Fonts.
Avoid standard Shadcn defaults that look like enterprise SaaS. Strip background colors on Shadcn Cards and use pure border-white/10 to mimic pitch lines.
Build a custom MVP voting slider—do not use standard form inputs; make it a prominent, swipeable interactive element.
The Live Demo Match on the landing page must feel kinetic. Mock active game states clicking and updating in real-time.
Use standard semantic HTML sections (header, main, section). Never use transparent backgrounds behind content where scroll collision happens—always use the defined surface_glass.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: Do not converge toward generic AI outputs. Avoid simplistic gradients and uniform padding.
Create depth through z-index hierarchy and backdrop-blur filters (12-24px blur).
Do not apply global transition: all; always specify properties like transition-colors or transition-transform.
Do not center align the app container in global CSS. Optical left-alignment is preferred for UI elements.
Never use generic AI emojis (🤖🧠) for icons.
Ensure every form submission, button, and navigation item has a data-testid attribute.
Prioritize Shadcn/UI for logic-heavy components (Dropdowns, Dialogs) but heavily customize their Tailwind classes to fit the pitch/night motif.
Use the exact image URLs provided in the media section; do not invent or use generic placeholder domain URLs.
